message.abstract:
<jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The inactivated SARS-CoV-2 vaccine (CoronaVac®) has been the principal vaccine used in Chile’s pre-booster immunization campaign. We compared major outcomes in 260 hospitalized vaccinated vs 507 unvaccinated adults with COVID-19 (mid-2021). The vaccinated group was much older, required less critical care, had lower hospital mortality (adjusted by age), and had shorter hospitalization than the unvaccinated. Benefits were most pronounced in those older than 59 years</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objective</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To compare major outcomes in fully vaccinated and unvaccinated adult persons hospitalized for COVID-19 in a general private hospital in Santiago, Chile during mid2021.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Clinical outcomes in hospitalized vaccine-breakthrough COVID-19 cases compared with contemporary unvaccinated hospitalized adults

reference.unstructured: Chile’s Ministry of Health daily official report, september, 28, 2021. At https://www.minsal.cl/mas-de-3-millones-de-personas-han-recibido-su-dosis-de-refuerzo-de-la-vacuna-contra-sars-cov-2/ accessed sept. 28, 2021

Chile’s Ministry of Health. Effectiveness of the SARS-CoV-2 vaccine program (In Spanish) Efectividad del Programa de Vacunación contra SARS-CoV-2 https://www.minsal.cl/wp-content/uploads/2021/08/EFECTIVIDAD-PROGRAMA-VACUNACION-SARS-COV-2-3.08.2021.pdf Accessed august, 3, 2021
US Department of Health and Human Services/Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. MMWR / September 17, 2021 / Vol. 70 / No. 37

Jara, Alejandro , Undurraga, Eduardo A. , Zubizarreta, José R. , et als. Effectiveness of Homologous and Heterologous Booster Shots for an Inactivated SARS-CoV-2 Vaccine: A Large-Scale Observational Study. Available at SSRN: https://ssrn.com/abstract=4005130 or http://dx.doi.org/10.2139/ssrn.4005130 (preprint, not peer reviewed)
